Title: Suppression of OsMADS7 in rice endosperm stabilizes amylose content under high temperature stress

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MADS-box transcription factor; class E floral organ identity gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation of starch/amylose accumulation under high temperature; likely transcriptional and post-transcriptional regulation of Wx/GBSSI expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: starch biosynthesis, specifically amylose accumulation/stability during endosperm filling under high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major starch component determining rice eating and grain quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of OsMADS7 in rice endosperm stabilizes amylose content under high temperature stress and reduces the heat-induced decline in amylose content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: heat-induced negative regulator of amylose stability in developing rice endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high temperature during early grain filling/milk stage
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: OsMADS7 suppression increased total Wx transcript, mature Wx transcript, and first-intron splicing efficiency under high temperature, implying OsMADS7 negatively regulates Wx expression at transcriptional and post-transcriptional levels
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsMADS8 (functional redundancy discussed, not direct interaction shown)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in floral organs (inflorescence, anther, pistil), seed and endosperm; highest in panicles; mainly expressed in early endosperm development (6–15 DAP); strongly induced (>6-fold) by high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm at early filling stage; induced by high temperature at milk stage
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature treatment 35°C day/28°C night versus room/control temperature 28°C day/22°C night
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive RNAi promoter: NA; endosperm-specific GluC promoter (endosperm-specific) used for suppression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive suppression reduced spikelet fertility, and heat further aggravated fertility loss; some endosperm-specific RNAi lines also showed lowered fertility, though line M7142 largely avoided this penalty
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown (constitutive and endosperm-specific); qRT-PCR; amylose quantification; Wx splicing analysis; grain filling analysis; DSC; fertility assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS7 is moderately expressed in endosperm and strongly induced by high temperature during early grain filling. 2. Constitutive or endosperm-specific RNAi suppression of OsMADS7 reduced the heat-induced decline in amylose content and increased DD-value, indicating improved amylose stability under heat stress. 3. OsMADS7 knockdown increased total and mature Wx/GBSSI transcripts and improved first-intron splicing efficiency under high temperature, suggesting OsMADS7 negatively regulates Wx. 4. Constitutive suppression caused major spikelet fertility penalties, whereas endosperm-specific suppression could retain quality benefits with reduced fertility cost in selected lines such as M7142.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable candidate for breeding heat-tolerant rice with stable amylose content and better eating quality; endosperm-specific down-regulation is preferable to avoid reproductive defects; line M7142 is highlighted as a promising resource
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (cited from Xu et al., 2015; accession not provided)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on stabilization of amylose content under heat rather than increasing amylose under normal conditions. Endosperm-specific suppression outperformed constitutive suppression in balancing grain quality and fertility.
